message.content: Expression: the grid where the three central columns are completely filled with Xs and both outer columns are entirely empty.

Actually target: rows each: □ X X X □. So column pattern: col1 empty, col2 X, col3 X, col4 X, col5 empty. So a solid 3-wide vertical block centered, spanning all rows. Distractor1 has Xs on right side columns 3-5, leftmost two columns empty. Distractor2 has Xs on left side columns 1-3, rightmost two empty. So distinguishing: target has Xs in middle three columns, not leftmost or rightmost columns. So "the grid with a vertical bar of Xs occupying the three central columns" or "the grid where the middle three columns are filled and the outer columns are empty". Must start with "Expression: ". Probably: "the grid with Xs in the three central columns and empty cells in the outer columns". Ensure distinguishing. Let's output.

Central three columns: columns 2,3,4 (if counting 1-5). Both outer columns (1 and5) empty. So pattern: column1 empty, column2 X, column3 X, column4 X, column5 empty.
